---
title: "Preparación"
format:  
  html:
    code-copy: true
    code-tools: true
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-expand: 2
    toc-title: Contents
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 4 
    fig-width: 10  
    fig-height: 7
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
editor: source
lang: es
fontsize: 12pt
---

```{r}
#| label: set
#| echo: false
#| messedad: false
#| warning: false

library(knitr)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, include = TRUE, warning = FALSE, messedad = FALSE)

table_format <- if(is_html_output()) {
  "html"
} else if(is_latex_output()) {
  "latex"
}
table_format2 <- if(is_html_output()) {
  T
} else if(is_latex_output()) {
  F
}

options(kableExtra.html.bsTable = T)
options(knitr.kable.NA = "")
```

```{r}
if (!require("pacman")) install.packedads("pacman") # instalar pacman
                            # cargar librerias
pacman::p_load(dplyr,       # Manipulacion de datos
               knitr,
               kableExtra,
               summarytools,
               ggplot2,
               ggeffects,
               haven,
               sjlabelled,
               lme4,
               sjmisc,
               sjPlot,
               ggrepel,
               ggpubr
               )

data_original<- haven::read_dta(here::here("input/original/OECD_RTM_2024.dta"))

data <- data_original %>%
  dplyr::select(id,
              country,
              ctrcode, 
              year, 
              ctryear, 
              weight, 
              sex=s2,
              age=s3_agegroup,
              educ=s7_cat3,
              income=s9_dec,
              employment=s25,
              selfemploy=s11,
              platform=s29,
              distributive=q16,
              unnemployment=q2b,
              tecnoestres_1=q25a,
              tecnoestres_2=q25b,
              tecnoestres_3=q25c,
              tecnoestres_4=q25d,
              tecnoestres_5=q25e,
              tecnoestres_6=q25f,
              tecnoestres_7=q25g,
              tecnoestres_8=q25h,
              tecnoestres_9=q25i,
              deservingness=q17              
              )

gdp_2024 <- tibble::tribble(
  ~ctrcode, ~gdp_2024,
  "AUT",  58373,
  "BEL",  56796,
  "CAN",  54283,
  "CHE", 108256,
  "CHL",  17203,
  "DEU",  56087,
  "DNK",  71214,
  "ESP",  36076,
  "EST",  31419,
  "FIN",  53189,
  "FRA",  46150,
  "GBR",  52637,
  "GRC",  24716,
  "IRL", 112356,
  "ISR",  54177,
  "ITA",  40226,
  "KOR",  36500,
  "LTU",  29379,
  "LVA",  23243,
  "MEX",  13826,
  "NLD",  67690,
  "NOR",  89684,
  "POL",  24973,
  "PRT",  27832,
  "SVN",  34103,
  "TUR",  15463,
  "USA",  86173
)

data <- data %>% set_na(.,na=c(98,99))

# generate analytical sample
digitalization <-
  data %>% #filter(ctrcode=="CHL") %>% 
  select(tecnoestres_1, tecnoestres_2, tecnoestres_3, tecnoestres_4, tecnoestres_5, 
          tecnoestres_6, tecnoestres_7, tecnoestres_8, tecnoestres_9 #tecnoestres
         )



vars <- paste0("tecnoestres_", 1:9)

labelled::var_label(digitalization)[vars] <- labelled::var_label(digitalization)[vars] %>%
  stringr::str_remove("^q25[a-z]:\\s*Digitaln\\. and your job:\\s*")


save(digitalization, file=here::here("input/proc/digitalization.RData"))


data <- data %>% rowwise() %>%
        mutate(tecnoestres = mean(c(tecnoestres_1, tecnoestres_3, tecnoestres_5), na.rm=TRUE),
               pos_tecnoestres = mean(c(tecnoestres_7, tecnoestres_8, tecnoestres_9), na.rm=TRUE),
               skills = tecnoestres_6
               )
data <- merge(data, gdp_2024, by="ctrcode")

# ============================================================
# Clasificación de regímenes de bienestar — versión revisada
# Fuentes principales:
#   Esping-Andersen (1990) Three Worlds of Welfare Capitalism
#   Ferrera (1996) Southern European regime
#   Aidukaite (2011) Post-communist welfare reforms
#   Fenger (2007) CEE typology
# ============================================================

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    welfare_regime = case_when(

      # 1. SOCIALDEMÓCRATA
      # Esping-Andersen (1990): universalismo, alta desmercantilización
      country %in% c(5,   # Denmark
                     7,   # Finland
                     19,  # Norway
                     18   # Netherlands
                     ) ~ "Social-democratic",

      # 2. LIBERAL
      # Esping-Andersen (1990): residual, focalizado, mercado
      # Martínez Franzoni (2008): Chile y México — liberal latinoamericano
      # Aidukaite (2011): bálticos con orientación liberal post-soviética
      country %in% c(3,   # Canada
                     11,  # Ireland
                     26,  # United Kingdom
                     27,  # United States
                     4,   # Chile
                     17,  # Mexico
                     6,   # Estonia
                     15,  # Latvia
                     16   # Lithuania
                     ) ~ "Liberal",

      # 3. CONSERVADOR-CORPORATIVISTA + SUR DE EUROPA
      # Esping-Andersen (1990) + Ferrera (1996)
      country %in% c(1,   # Austria
                     2,   # Belgium
                     8,   # France
                     9,   # Germany
                     24,  # Switzerland
                     10,  # Greece
                     13,  # Italy
                     21,  # Portugal
                     23,  # Spain
                     12,  # Israel
                     14,  # Korea
                     25,   # Türkiye
                     20,  # Poland
                     22   # Slovenia
                     ) ~ "Conservative",

      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    welfare_regime = factor(welfare_regime,
                            levels = c("Liberal",
                                       "Social-democratic",
                                       "Conservative"))
  )

data$sex <- as_factor(data$sex)
data$age <- as_factor(data$age)
data$educ <- as_factor(data$educ)
data$selfemploy <- as_factor(data$selfemploy)
data$platform <- as_factor(data$platform)
data$platform <- car::recode(data$platform, "2=1;1=2;3=2")
data$selfemploy <- car::recode(data$selfemploy, "1=1;2=2;3=2")

save(data, file=here::here("input/proc/data.RData"))
```

```{r}
distributive <- data %>% select(distributive, country, ctrcode, welfare_regime, gdp_2024)



country_data <- distributive %>% group_by(ctrcode) %>%
                      summarise(distributive = mean(distributive, na.rm=TRUE),
                                gdp_2024 = mean(gdp_2024, na.rm=TRUE))

save(country_data, file=here::here("input/proc/country_data.RData"))
```
